user_scenarios.user_goal: As a crawler workbench user, manage domain channels and child sources from the frontend without leaking secret values.
prerequisites: Playwright browser runtime is installed for npm run test:ui.
The UI scenario uses mocked or isolated backend data and synthetic secrets only.
steps: Navigate to the Domain Channels page from the primary navigation.
Select a domain and view its channel table.
Create a channel with base URL, kind, connector, auth mode, trust, enabled state, and notes.
Select the channel and verify the detail panel shows editable channel fields, source count, secret status, child sources, and probe history.
Replace a synthetic secret and confirm the input is cleared after submission.
Run Verify Access and inspect latest probe status plus probe history.
Create a child source under the selected channel and confirm it appears in the child source list.
Navigate to existing Sources and Queue pages and confirm they still render.
expected_outcomes: The Domain Channels page renders domains, channel search/filter controls, a channel table, and a right detail panel.
Create and edit channel actions send the expected API payloads.
Secret replacement never renders the plaintext secret after submission.
Probe history and latest probe status are visible to the user.
A child source can be created under a selected channel.
Existing Sources and Queue navigation still render.
failure_signals: The Domain Channels navigation item is missing.
Channel create/edit controls do not send required fields.
Synthetic secret plaintext remains visible after submission.
Probe history is missing from the selected channel detail panel.
Existing Sources or Queue pages fail to render.
cleanup: Remove generated Playwright output if a clean rerun is needed.
